touches.ai_summary: Brief cold call with Charlotte King (Director of CS). Rep introduced AskElephant's CRM automation capabilities. Charlotte expressed interest in solving manual data entry bottlenecks in their CS workflows and agreed to a follow-up email.

questions_asked.question: Are you currently facing challenges with CRM data entry or automation in your customer success processes?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, our team spends hours manually updating deal stages and logging customer interactions.

next_steps: Send follow-up email with product overview and case studies

touches.ai_summary: Follow-up email sent with AskElephant overview, fintech case study, and proposed qualification call agenda. Charlotte responded confirming interest and availability for next week.

questions_asked.question: Does your solution integrate with Salesforce and our existing tech stack?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we have native Salesforce integration and work with most common fintech tools.

next_steps: Schedule qualification call for next Tuesday 2pm

touches.ai_summary: 18-minute qualification call with Charlotte. Discussed CoralBridge's current CRM pain points: manual call logging, inconsistent data quality, and CS team spending 10+ hours/week on admin tasks. Charlotte mentioned they're also evaluating Fathom but likes AskElephant's automation depth. Qualified budget range and timeline (looking to implement within 60 days). Team size: 26 CS/sales users.

questions_asked.question: What's driving the urgency to solve this now?
questions_asked.answer: We're scaling fast and the manual overhead is becoming unsustainable. Our CEO wants better pipeline visibility.

questions_asked.question: How does AskElephant compare to Fathom in terms of automation capabilities?
questions_asked.answer: AskElephant offers deeper workflow automation and custom AI models, while Fathom focuses more on transcription and note-taking.

questions_asked.question: Who else should be involved in the evaluation process?
questions_asked.answer: Our CEO Miles Sanchez will need to sign off, and possibly our RevOps lead.

touches.ai_summary: Pre-demo email sent with tailored agenda focusing on CRM automation workflows, Salesforce integration, and CS team productivity gains. Charlotte confirmed demo attendance and shared additional context about their tech stack.
Demo rescheduled by Charlotte due to conflicting board prep meeting. Moved to following Monday.
touches.reschedule_reason: prospect asked to push by a week

touches.ai_summary: 44-minute product demo with Charlotte and RevOps lead. Walked through AI call summarization, automated CRM updates, deal health scoring, and Salesforce workflows. Charlotte particularly engaged with automation builder and asked detailed questions about custom AI training. Mentioned Fathom's pricing came in lower but acknowledged AskElephant's broader capabilities. Discussed consulting package for implementation support.

questions_asked.question: Can we train the AI on our specific fintech compliance language and deal stages?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, the consulting package includes custom AI model training on your terminology and workflows.

questions_asked.question: What's the typical implementation timeline with consulting support?
questions_asked.answer: With our automations plus consulting package, most customers are fully live within 4-6 weeks.

questions_asked.question: How does our pricing compare to what you're seeing from Fathom?
questions_asked.answer: Fathom came in about 30% lower, but they don't offer the automation or consulting we need.

objections: Pricing is higher than Fathom's proposal
next_steps: Set up exec meeting with Miles Sanchez

touches.ai_summary: 41-minute executive alignment call with Miles Sanchez (CEO) and Charlotte. Discussed strategic value: improving pipeline visibility, reducing CS admin burden, and scaling revenue operations. Miles focused on ROI and asked about competitive differentiation vs Fathom. Charlotte advocated for AskElephant's automation depth. Miles expressed concern about budget but acknowledged the business case. Requested revised pricing proposal.

questions_asked.question: What ROI are other fintech customers seeing in terms of time savings and revenue impact?
questions_asked.answer: Typical fintech customers save 12-15 hours per rep per week and see 15-20% faster deal cycles due to better data hygiene.

questions_asked.question: Can you help us justify the price difference versus Fathom to our finance team?
questions_asked.answer: Happy to provide a detailed ROI analysis. The automation and consulting capabilities typically deliver 3-4x the value of basic transcription tools.

questions_asked.question: What's your timeline for making a decision?
questions_asked.answer: We'd like to have something in place by end of quarter, so ideally decide within 2-3 weeks.

objections: Budget is tight this quarter, need to justify premium pricing over Fathom
next_steps: Send ROI analysis and updated proposal
Charlotte to build internal business case

touches.ai_summary: 24-minute pricing negotiation with Miles and Charlotte. Discussed discount options and contract terms. Miles pushed for additional discount citing Fathom's lower pricing and budget constraints. Rep offered 30% discount in exchange for annual prepay and case study participation. Miles seemed receptive but wanted to review internally with finance.

questions_asked.question: Can you match Fathom's pricing or get closer to their range?
questions_asked.answer: We can't match on price alone, but I can offer 30% off with annual prepay, which brings us much closer while delivering significantly more value.

questions_asked.question: What flexibility do you have on payment terms?
questions_asked.answer: With annual prepay we can offer the 30% discount. Quarterly would be 20% discount maximum.

questions_asked.question: If we can land on pricing, are there any other blockers to moving forward?
questions_asked.answer: No, pricing is the main hurdle. If we can make the numbers work, we're ready to proceed.

next_steps: Send updated contract with 30% discount and annual terms
Miles to review with finance by end of week

touches.ai_summary: 38-minute negotiation call rescheduled twice due to Miles' travel, finally conducted with Charlotte. Reviewed final contract terms and discount structure (31.7% final discount approved). Charlotte confirmed Miles signed off on budget and they're ready to proceed pending legal review. Discussed implementation kickoff timeline and consulting engagement scope. Charlotte flagged minor concerns about onboarding bandwidth but overall positive on moving forward.

questions_asked.question: Can we phase the implementation to reduce onboarding burden on our team?
questions_asked.answer: Yes, we can break it into two phases: core automation first, then advanced workflows and custom AI training in phase two.

questions_asked.question: What does legal review typically take on your end?
questions_asked.answer: Usually 3-5 business days. Our team is pretty fast.

objections: Concerned about team bandwidth for onboarding during busy quarter
next_steps: Send final contract to Charlotte for legal review
Schedule implementation kickoff for week after contract signature
touches.reschedule_reason: prospect asked to push by a week
